demographics.living_situation: resident in a residential aged-care facility (nursing home); totally bedbound, needs a two-person assist to be moved

contact_nok.relationship: nursing home staff / next of kin

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: The sore on his lower back has suddenly started smelling terrible, it is leaking yellow pus, and he is burning up with a fever. He has gone confused since this morning too. (Call made by the nursing staff looking after him.)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Over the last 12 hours the long-standing sore on his tailbone has changed suddenly — bad smell, thick yellow discharge — and he has spiked a fever and become confused. Staff are worried and want to know what to do now.

symptom_narrative.onset: abrupt change over the past 12 hours in a patient whose sore had been stable
symptom_narrative.character: the sore is now leaking a lot of thick yellow pus and smells very bad; the skin around it looks red and feels hot; he is feverish, sweaty and flushed, and has become confused and drowsy
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: staff say this is a sudden and severe change — he is groaning whenever they move him and is not himself at all
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: a pressure sore over the tailbone / lower back that he has had for a while
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: came on quickly over roughly half a day and is getting worse
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing so far; paracetamol was given a couple of hours ago for the fever
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any repositioning or turning causes him a lot of pain
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: high fever, sweating, flushing, new confusion/drowsiness, fast breathing, pain on being moved
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: he has had the pressure sore for some time but it had been stable and had never smelled or discharged like this before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: totally bedbound at baseline; now also confused and difficult to rouse

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a long-standing pressure sore (bedsore) over his tailbone / lower back
past_medical_history.how_managed: dressings and regular turning by nursing staff; had been stable until today

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: prolonged immobility — he is confined to bed and cannot move himself
problems with bladder control (incontinence), so his pad is often wet

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: given a couple of hours ago for the fever

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives in a residential aged-care facility, bedbound, fully dependent on nursing staff for all care and repositioning; history for this call is given entirely by staff because he is too confused to answer

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: staff-measured with a ward device; a paracetamol dose 2 hours earlier may be partly masking the fever
single staff-measured reading; low for an older adult and needs confirmation

objective_data_offered.value: staff show the sore over the tailbone leaking thick yellow pus with a strong foul smell; the skin around it looks red, shiny and swollen
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: a lay/on-camera view of the wound surface only; the depth, wound bed and true extent cannot be judged over video

objective_data_offered.value: he is drowsy, confused, flushed and sweaty, and groans whenever he is moved
the alternating air-pressure mattress looks under-inflated and the incontinence pad is heavily soaked
